description: A talking/animatronic skull prop: up to six independently-toggleable servo axes (jaw, pan, tilt, nod, eye up/down, eye left/right), four 3D OBJ meshes (head, jaw, left eye, right eye), a fixed per-axis orientation offset for each enabled axis, and an optional RGBW color ability for eye lighting. Source: src-core/models/DMX/DmxSkull.{h,cpp} (DisplayAs = DisplayAsType::DmxSkull; constructor attaches `color_ability = std::make_unique<DmxColorAbilityRGB>()` unconditionally, but its use is further gated by the HasColor flag below). No shutter/dimmer/beam ability.

childElements.name: faceInfo
childElements.description: Singing face definitions. CORRECTION vs the other seven DMX documents: DmxSkullPropertyAdapter::DisableUnusedProperties disables ModelStringType, ModelStringColor and ModelDimmingCurves, but - unlike the base DmxPropertyAdapter::DisableUnusedProperties (inherited by every other Dmx*PropertyAdapter) - it does NOT disable ModelFaces. faceInfo therefore remains fully enabled/editable in DmxSkull's property grid, unlike every other DMX document in this family.

childElements.name: stateInfo
childElements.description: State definitions. Deliberately left enabled for DMX models.

childElements.name: subModel
childElements.description: Submodel definitions. Not disabled for DMX models.

childElements.name: dimmingCurve
childElements.description: Dimming curve. Round-tripped but disabled in DmxSkull's property grid (ModelDimmingCurves is disabled the same as every other DMX document).

childElements.name: Aliases
childElements.description: Optional wrapper containing one or more <alias name="..."/> children: alternate historical names for this model, used to match renamed models on import/mapping (BaseSerializingVisitor::WriteAliases / XmlDeserializingModelFactory::DeserializeAliases). Not yet described by a dedicated shared fragment in this metadata tree.

childElements.name: JawServo
childElements.description: Jaw axis servo child element (fixed, axis-named - NOT part of the {n}-numbered Servo1/Servo2/... family used by DmxServo/DmxServo3d). Instantiates the Servo pattern documented in shared/DmxAbilities.json notes. Only created/present when HasJaw() is true (DmxSkull::CreateServo("JawServo")).

childElements.name: PanServo
childElements.description: Pan axis servo child element. Same attribute set as JawServo. Only present when HasPan() is true.

childElements.name: TiltServo
childElements.description: Tilt axis servo child element. Same attribute set as JawServo. Only present when HasTilt() is true.

childElements.name: NodServo
childElements.description: Nod axis servo child element. Same attribute set as JawServo. Only present when HasNod() is true.

childElements.name: EyeUpDownServo
childElements.description: Eye up/down axis servo child element. CORRECTION vs the task brief's shorthand "EyeUD": the actual literal element/base name is "EyeUpDownServo" (DmxSkull::CreateServo("EyeUpDownServo"), XmlDeserializingModelFactory::DeserializeDmxSkull `if ("EyeUpDownServo" == name)`), not "EyeUD" or "EyeUDServo". Same attribute set as JawServo. Only present when HasEyeUD() is true.

childElements.name: EyeLeftRightServo
childElements.description: Eye left/right axis servo child element. CORRECTION vs the task brief's shorthand "EyeLR": the actual literal element/base name is "EyeLeftRightServo", not "EyeLR" or "EyeLRServo". Same attribute set as JawServo. Only present when HasEyeLR() is true.

childElements.name: HeadMesh
childElements.description: Head 3D mesh child element (fixed, axis-named). Instantiates the Mesh pattern documented in shared/DmxAbilities.json notes. Always created (DmxSkull::CreateMesh("HeadMesh")).

childElements.name: JawMesh
childElements.description: Jaw 3D mesh child element. Same attribute set as HeadMesh. Always created.

childElements.name: EyeMeshL
childElements.description: Left eye 3D mesh child element. Same attribute set as HeadMesh. Always created.

childElements.name: EyeMeshR
childElements.description: Right eye 3D mesh child element. Same attribute set as HeadMesh. Always created.

properties.description: Whether this skull has a jaw servo axis at all (DmxSkull::HasJaw()/SetHasJaw()). Configured via the "Skull Config" popup dialog (SkullConfigDialog checkboxes), not a direct top-level grid row; gates whether the JawServo child element and its DmxJawOrient property exist/are shown.
Whether this skull has a pan servo axis (DmxSkull::HasPan()/SetHasPan()). Configured via the Skull Config dialog; gates PanServo/DmxPanOrient.
Whether this skull has a tilt servo axis (DmxSkull::HasTilt()/SetHasTilt()). Configured via the Skull Config dialog; gates TiltServo/DmxTiltOrient.
Whether this skull has a nod servo axis (DmxSkull::HasNod()/SetHasNod()). Configured via the Skull Config dialog; gates NodServo/DmxNodOrient.
Whether this skull has an eye up/down servo axis (DmxSkull::HasEyeUD()/SetHasEyeUD()). Configured via the Skull Config dialog; gates EyeUpDownServo/DmxEyeUDOrient.
Whether this skull has an eye left/right servo axis (DmxSkull::HasEyeLR()/SetHasEyeLR()). Configured via the Skull Config dialog; gates EyeLeftRightServo/DmxEyeLROrient.

Whether this skull's eyes have an RGBW color ability at all (DmxSkull::HasColor()/SetHasColor()). Configured via the Skull Config dialog; gates whether the "Color Properties" category (DmxEyeBrtChannel + shared/DmxAbilities.json's color-ability properties) is shown/used at all - note this is a coarser, DmxSkull-specific on/off switch layered on top of (and independent from) DmxModel::HasColorAbility(), which is always true for DmxSkull since its constructor always creates the color_ability object regardless of this flag.
When "1", servo channels are treated as 16-bit (coarse+fine) pairs (DmxSkull::Is16Bit()/SetIs16Bit()). Configured via the Skull Config dialog's "16 bits" checkbox.

When "1", renders only the OBJ meshes without the servo/orientation gizmo overlays (DmxSkull::IsMeshOnly()/SetMeshOnly()). Has its own direct top-level grid checkbox (unlike the Has*/Bits16 flags above, which are all Skull-Config-dialog-only).
DMX channel controlling eye brightness/dimming (DmxSkull::GetEyeBrightnessChannel()/SetEyeBrightnessChannel()). Only shown in the property grid when HasColor() is true.

Fixed orientation offset (degrees) applied to the jaw servo's rendered position (DmxSkull::GetJawOrient()/SetJawOrient()). Default is DmxSkull::default_orient[JAW] = 0, used as the read-fallback via GetDefaultOrient(SERVO_TYPE::JAW). Only present/shown when HasJaw() is true.
Fixed orientation offset (degrees) for the pan servo (DmxSkull::GetPanOrient()/SetPanOrient()). Default is default_orient[PAN] = 90. Only present/shown when HasPan() is true.
Fixed orientation offset (degrees) for the tilt servo (DmxSkull::GetTiltOrient()/SetTiltOrient()). Default is default_orient[TILT] = -20. Only present/shown when HasTilt() is true.
Fixed orientation offset (degrees) for the nod servo (DmxSkull::GetNodOrient()/SetNodOrient()). Default is default_orient[NOD] = 29. Only present/shown when HasNod() is true.
Fixed orientation offset (degrees) for the eye up/down servo (DmxSkull::GetEyeUDOrient()/SetEyeUDOrient()). Default is default_orient[EYE_UD] = 35. Only present/shown when HasEyeUD() is true.
Fixed orientation offset (degrees) for the eye left/right servo (DmxSkull::GetEyeLROrient()/SetEyeLROrient()). Default is default_orient[EYE_LR] = -35. Only present/shown when HasEyeLR() is true.

notes: No shutter/dimmer/beam ability: DmxSkull attaches only a color_ability (unconditionally, RGBW) plus the always-present preset ability - no shutter_ability/dimmer_ability/beam_ability - so only DmxChannelCount, DmxColorType and the RGBW-channel properties from shared/DmxAbilities.json apply beyond this document's own properties (color type is fixed at RGBW like DmxGeneral/DmxFloodlight/DmxFloodArea - never switched dynamically).

gridKey omitted for HasPan/HasTilt/HasNod/HasEyeUD/HasEyeLR/HasColor/Bits16/HasJaw above: none of the Has*/Bits16 flags have a direct property-grid row of their own - they are only editable through the "Skull Config" popup-dialog checkboxes (SkullConfigDialog), which read/write the model directly rather than going through a named wxPGProperty/gridKey.

Servo child-element base names use full descriptive names (JawServo/PanServo/TiltServo/NodServo/EyeUpDownServo/EyeLeftRightServo), not the short axis labels (Jaw/Pan/Tilt/Nod/EyeUD/EyeLR) the task brief's survey shorthand suggested - see the childElements entries above for the verified literal names.
